atom.description: Recomposition is the runtime mechanism that keeps the UI consistent with state. When a snapshot read inside a composable changes, the runtime invalidates that scope and re-runs it. Recomposition can happen at any time, in any order, often, and may be cancelled mid-way. Code that assumes a composable runs 'once' or 'exactly when the user pressed the button' is wrong; the only contract is 'the body returns the latest projection of inputs to UI'.
